title: Fix CTkEntry place_holder
body:
This PR aims to fix placeholder behavior when a textvariable is assigned to a CTkEntry and introduce a controlled toggle for the textvariable callback event.

Key changes expected:
- Ensure the placeholder logic works correctly even if a textvariable is bound to the CTkEntry.
- Add a flag to enable/disable the textvariable callback to prevent unintended placeholder activations while the placeholder is shown or while programmatic updates occur.
- Guard the textvariable callback so that placeholder logic is not triggered during placeholder display, and re-enable it when the placeholder is deactivated.
- Preserve existing UI behavior when no textvariable is bound or when textvariable contains non-empty values.

Potential tests to consider:
- Placeholder appears when textvariable is "" or None and placeholder_text is provided.
- Placeholder does not appear when textvariable has a non-empty value.
- Setting textvariable programmatically to "" triggers placeholder activation only if allowed by the new flag.
- Placeholder deactivation re-enables textvariable callback and behaves as expected when user types or clears text.
- Ensure no regression for normal CTkEntry usage without textvariable.

step_plan.summary: Fix CTkEntry placeholder behavior when a textvariable is bound by introducing a guarded textvariable callback and a toggle to enable/disable the callback during placeholder activation/deactivation.

steps.operation: Add _textvariable_callback_enabled flag in CTkEntry.__init__ and ensure existing textvariable binding remains intact
steps.reason: Introduce a global flag that controls whether the textvariable callback should run, enabling safe gating of updates during placeholder activation without changing existing default behavior.
steps.side_effects: Adds an initialization path for the flag; minimal risk to startup; ensures backward compatibility when no textvariable is bound; may require updating any initialization tests if they rely on default callback state.

steps.operation: Guard CTkEntry._textvariable_callback execution with the new enable flag
steps.reason: Prevent placeholder logic from triggering while the callback is disabled or during placeholder display; preserve normal behavior when enabled.
steps.side_effects: Callback will return early when disabled, avoiding placeholder interference during certain states; downstream behavior remains unchanged when enabled; may require tests for bound-variable changes during placeholder visibility.

steps.operation: Disable the textvariable callback during placeholder activation in CTkEntry._activate_placeholder
steps.reason: Guard against unintended updates from a bound textvariable while the placeholder is being shown, ensuring placeholder determinism.
steps.side_effects: Bound variable changes won’t trigger placeholder logic during activation; ensures consistent placeholder display; may require tests to cover activation paths with bound variables.

steps.operation: Re-enable the textvariable callback after placeholder deactivation in CTkEntry._deactivate_placeholder
steps.reason: Restore normal behavior so subsequent user input or programmatic updates are processed via the textvariable callback after the placeholder is hidden.
steps.side_effects: Normal callback flow resumes post-deactivation; tests should verify programmatic updates and user input re-trigger as expected; minimizes regression for standard CTkEntry usage.
